title: MRL Increment 3: Expose Master Requirements List (Read-Only via Application Layer)
body:
# MRL Increment 3: Expose Master Requirements List (Read-Only via Application Layer)

## Objective

Introduce a read-only application-layer interface for accessing Master Requirements List (MRL) entries. This increment enables application-layer integration without introducing persistence, UI, or workflow concerns.

In this context, MRL entries are represented by the `QualificationRequirement` domain entity introduced in Increment 1.

## Background

- The domain model (`QualificationRequirement`) and enums were defined in Increment 1.
- An in-memory domain service (`InMemoryQualificationRequirementService`) was implemented in Increment 2.
- This increment focuses on clean, read-only exposure of MRL entries through an application-facing provider interface.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Define a new application-layer interface: `IMasterRequirementsReadService`
  - Methods must include:
    - `GetAll()`
    - `GetByType(RequirementType type)`
    - `GetByApprovalStatus(RequirementApprovalStatus status)`
- Implement the interface via a concrete class: `InMemoryMasterRequirementsReadService`
  - The provider must delegate all data access to `InMemoryQualificationRequirementService` and must not maintain its own data collection.
  - The provider must not re-implement filtering logic against its own data store.
- Wire up basic unit tests for the new provider

### Out of Scope

- UI exposure
- API wiring
- Authorization, roles, or permissions
- Persistence or database implementation
- DI wiring

## Deliverables

- Interface: `IMasterRequirementsReadService`
  - Read-only contract with methods described above
- Implementation: `InMemoryMasterRequirementsReadService`
  - Delegates to `InMemoryQualificationRequirementService`
- Unit tests:
  - Validate basic query behavior (not coverage of `InMemoryQualificationRequirementService` itself)
  - Assert that the service does not mutate or persist state (read-only behavior)
- The provider is application-facing and read-only; it does not replace or duplicate the underlying domain service.

## Naming Clarification

This interface represents an application-layer, read-only access point and is not the authoritative domain service.

## Outcome

- Enable UI prototyping and integration (read-only)
- Maintain strict separation of domain vs application logic
- Avoid premature commitments to persistence or workflow logic
